---
title: "Health of Canadians"
description: "Life expectancy and the conditions that shape it — Canada and OECD peers"
---

How long Canadians live, and the behaviours and conditions that shape it: life
expectancy, its recent decline, and population-health risk factors, for Canada and
17 OECD peers. For what the system spends and the capacity behind it — beds,
doctors, imaging, and wait times — see [The Health-Care System](health-care-system.qmd).

```{python}
#| echo: false
import os
import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
from pipeline.config import (
    CANADA_COLOR, COMPARATOR_COLORS, PEER_COLOR, HIGHLIGHT_WIDTH, PEER_WIDTH,
    HIGHLIGHT_COUNTRY, PEER_COUNTRIES, get_data_date,
)
from pipeline.charts import peer_comparison_line, ranked_bar, single_line

def show_peer_line(path, value_col, yaxis_title, source, initial_start=None, topical=None,
                   initial_visible=None):
    """Standard 17-peer line chart for a World Bank health series.

    `topical` (list of country codes) opens those non-comparator peers already
    coloured + visible — used where the prose singles a country out (e.g. South
    Korea on suicide); every other grey peer is still one legend click away."""
    if not os.path.exists(path):
        print("Data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
        return
    df = pd.read_csv(path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", value_col, title="", yaxis_title=yaxis_title,
        show_average=True, initial_start=initial_start, topical=topical,
        initial_visible=initial_visible,
        source_note=f"Source: {source} | Data as of: {get_data_date(path)}")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
```

## Where Canada stands

A scorecard of where Canada ranks among the 17 peers on each measure (latest available year). Dots are ordered by rank, so **further right is more favourable for Canada** (lower-is-better measures are flipped accordingly). Canada is red, key comparators in colour; hover a dot for its value and rank.

```{python}
#| echo: false
from pipeline.charts import page_snapshot
page_snapshot("health").show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
```

## Life expectancy at birth

Life expectancy at birth — the average number of years a newborn
could expect to live if current mortality rates held. It is the most widely used
summary measure of a population's health.

```{python}
#| echo: false
le_path = "data/health/oecd_life_expectancy.csv"
if os.path.exists(le_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(le_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(df, "year", "life_expectancy",
        title="", yaxis_title="Years", show_average=True, initial_visible=["USA", "AUS", "DEU"])
    fig.update_layout(annotations=[dict(
        text=f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(le_path)}",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.34,
        showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Life expectancy data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Latest life expectancy: ranked

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(le_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(le_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "life_expectancy", "Years",
        f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(le_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f} years<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## The recent decline

For most of the post-war era, life expectancy rose almost every year. That changed in 2020: Canada's fell from 82.2 years in 2019 to 81.3 in 2022 — the first sustained decline in decades — pushed down by COVID-19 and the [drug-toxicity crisis](substance-use.qmd). It edged back to 81.7 in 2023. The fall was milder than in the United States, shown here for contrast, where the decline was steeper and recovery slower.

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(le_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(le_path)
    fig = go.Figure()
    for code, nm, color, width in [("USA", "United States", COMPARATOR_COLORS["USA"], 2),
                                   ("CAN", "Canada", CANADA_COLOR, 3)]:
        d = df[(df.country_code == code) & (df.year >= 2000)].sort_values("year")
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=d.year, y=d.life_expectancy, mode="lines+markers",
            name=nm, line=dict(color=color, width=width), marker=dict(size=5),
            hovertemplate=nm + ": %{y:.1f} yrs (%{x})<extra></extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(plot_bgcolor="white", hovermode="x unified",
        legend=dict(orientation="h", y=1.04, x=0),
        yaxis=dict(title="Years", gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        xaxis=dict(title="", gridcolor="#e0e0e0"),
        margin=dict(t=40, b=120),
        annotations=[dict(text=f"Source: OECD Health Statistics | Data as of: {get_data_date(le_path)}",
            xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.18, showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
```

## Population health & risk factors

Life expectancy summarises the overall outcome; the measures here look upstream — at the behaviours and conditions that shape it, and at mental health. They come from the World Bank's compilation of World Health Organization, UN and International Diabetes Federation data, so all 17 peers are reported on a common basis. By default each chart shows Canada, the named comparators and the peer average; use the legend to add the other peers. Tobacco, alcohol and cannabis are covered in depth on the [Substance Use](substance-use.qmd) page.

## Suicide

Deaths by suicide per 100,000 population. Canada sits mid-pack among peers at roughly 9 per 100,000 — far below South Korea, the peer-group high, but above Mediterranean peers such as Italy and Israel.

```{python}
#| echo: false
show_peer_line("data/health/worldbank_suicide.csv", "suicide_rate",
    "Deaths per 100,000", "World Bank / WHO Global Health Observatory",
    initial_visible=["USA", "GBR"])
```

### Who is most affected, by age and sex

The single national rate hides a stark and consistent pattern: in every age group, suicide is far more common among **men** than women — about three times as often overall — and among men the rate climbs through midlife and stays high into older age. Women's rates are lower and flatter across the life course. (Age-specific deaths per 100,000 for the most recent year; rates for the youngest groups rest on small counts.)

```{python}
#| echo: false
sba_path = "data/health/statcan_suicide_by_age.csv"
if os.path.exists(sba_path):
    sba = pd.read_csv(sba_path)
    yr = int(sba["year"].max())
    cur = sba[sba["year"] == yr]
    order = ["15–19", "20–24", "25–29", "30–34", "35–39", "40–44", "45–49", "50–54",
             "55–59", "60–64", "65–69", "70–74", "75–79", "80–84", "85–89", "90+"]
    fig = go.Figure()
    for sex, color in [("Men", "#4477aa"), ("Women", "#ee8866")]:
        s = cur[cur["sex"] == sex].set_index("age_group").reindex(order).reset_index()
        fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(x=s["age_group"], y=s["rate"], name=sex, mode="lines+markers",
            line=dict(color=color, width=2.5), marker=dict(size=6),
            hovertemplate=sex + ", %{x}: %{y:.1f} per 100,000<extra></extra>"))
    fig.update_layout(plot_bgcolor="white", height=440, hovermode="x unified",
        yaxis=dict(title="Deaths per 100,000 population", gridcolor="#e0e0e0", rangemode="tozero"),
        xaxis=dict(title="Age group", gridcolor="#e0e0e0", tickangle=0),
        legend=dict(orientation="h", yanchor="bottom", y=1.02, x=0),
        margin=dict(t=40, b=80))
    fig.add_annotation(text=f"Source: Statistics Canada, Table 13-10-0392-01 — {yr}.",
        xref="paper", yref="paper", x=0, y=-0.20, showarrow=False, font=dict(size=10, color="#999"))
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Suicide-by-age data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

## Overweight

Share of adults with a body-mass index of 25 or higher. The measure has climbed across nearly every peer over three decades; Canada is around 60%, below the United States but above most of Europe and well above Japan and South Korea.

```{python}
#| echo: false
show_peer_line("data/health/worldbank_overweight.csv", "overweight_pct",
    "% of adults (BMI ≥ 25)", "World Bank / WHO Global Health Observatory",
    topical=["JPN", "KOR"])
```

## Diabetes

Diabetes is one of the most common chronic diseases and a major contributor to heart disease, kidney failure and other serious complications. This shows estimated prevalence among adults aged 20–79. The International Diabetes Federation publishes these at intervals rather than annually, so this is the latest available estimate, shown as a ranked comparison rather than a trend.

```{python}
#| echo: false
dm_path = "data/health/worldbank_diabetes.csv"
if os.path.exists(dm_path):
    df = pd.read_csv(dm_path)
    fig = ranked_bar(df, "diabetes_pct", "% of adults 20–79",
        f"Source: World Bank / IDF Diabetes Atlas | Data as of: {get_data_date(dm_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.1f}%<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Maternal mortality

Maternal deaths per 100,000 live births (modelled estimates on a consistent international basis). Most peers cluster at very low ratios — a peer median around 4 — but Canada, at roughly 12, sits near the high end of the group; only the United States is higher.

```{python}
#| echo: false
show_peer_line("data/health/worldbank_maternal_mortality.csv", "maternal_mortality",
    "Per 100,000 live births", "World Bank (WHO/UNICEF/UNFPA/World Bank/UNDESA)",
    initial_visible=["USA", "DEU", "GBR"])
```

## Childhood vaccination

The share of children aged 12–23 months immunized against measles (first dose).
Because measles is so contagious, roughly **95%** coverage — the dashed line — is
the widely used threshold for stopping community spread. Canada's coverage sits
around 90%, below most European peers, and coverage dipped across many countries
around the pandemic. (The matching DTP3 series is in the downloads.)

```{python}
#| echo: false
vac_path = "data/health/worldbank_measles_vaccination.csv"
if os.path.exists(vac_path):
    vac = pd.read_csv(vac_path)
    fig = peer_comparison_line(vac, "year", "measles_immunization_pct", "",
        "% of children 12–23 months", show_average=True,
        source_note=f"Source: World Bank (WHO/UNICEF immunization estimates) | Data as of: {get_data_date(vac_path)}")
    fig.add_hline(y=95, line_dash="dash", line_color="#555", line_width=1,
                  annotation_text="95% — herd immunity", annotation_position="top left",
                  annotation_font=dict(size=10, color="#555"))
    fig.update_yaxes(range=[50, 101])
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False, "modeBarButtonsToRemove": ["lasso2d", "select2d"]})
else:
    print("Vaccination data not yet available. Run the data pipeline.")
```

```{python}
#| echo: false
if os.path.exists(vac_path):
    fig = ranked_bar(vac, "measles_immunization_pct", "% of children 12–23 months",
        f"Source: World Bank (WHO/UNICEF immunization estimates) | Data as of: {get_data_date(vac_path)}",
        hover_template="%{y}: %{x:.0f}%<extra></extra>")
    fig.show(config={"displaylogo": False})
```

## Download data

- [Life Expectancy at Birth (CSV)](../data/health/oecd_life_expectancy.csv) — OECD Health Statistics
- [Suicide Mortality Rate (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_suicide.csv) — World Bank / WHO Global Health Observatory
- [Suicide Rate by Age & Sex (CSV)](../data/health/statcan_suicide_by_age.csv) — Statistics Canada Table 13-10-0392-01
- [Overweight (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_overweight.csv) — World Bank / WHO Global Health Observatory
- [Diabetes Prevalence (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_diabetes.csv) — World Bank / IDF Diabetes Atlas
- [Maternal Mortality (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_maternal_mortality.csv) — World Bank (WHO/UNICEF/UNFPA/World Bank/UNDESA)
- [Measles Immunization (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_measles_vaccination.csv) — World Bank (WHO/UNICEF estimates)
- [DTP3 Immunization (CSV)](../data/health/worldbank_dtp_vaccination.csv) — World Bank (WHO/UNICEF estimates)

System spending, capacity and access data are on [The Health-Care System](health-care-system.qmd).
